mnemonics: Half Adder Adds Two Bits (HAA2B) - Produces Sum and Carry (PSC)
Full Adder Feeds Three (FAF3) - Adds A, B, and Carry-in (ABC)
keyPoints: A combinational circuit's output depends ONLY on the current input combination.
Multiplexers (MUX) select one of many inputs; Demultiplexers (DEMUX) do the opposite.
Decoders activate a single output line based on the input code; Encoders do the reverse.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that combinational circuits have NO memory (no feedback loops).
Confusing the function of a decoder (input code to active output) with a demultiplexer (routes one input to one of many outputs).
